title: E2E test suite for player lifecycle and season-scoped points
description: Comprehensive Playwright E2E tests covering the full player deactivation lifecycle and season-scoped points table. Tests must seed multi-season data to verify cross-season isolation. This is the safety net ensuring all edge cases are covered — particularly that historical match data is never lost and points are correctly scoped.
acceptance_criteria: Test seed data includes two complete seasons with overlapping players
Test seed includes a player who played season 1 but left before season 2
Test seed includes a player who joined in season 2 only
Test seed includes a player who played both seasons
Points table tests verify season isolation (no cross-season bleed)
Points table tests verify departed player does not appear in new season
Points table tests verify Division 4 rule applies per-season
Deactivation tests verify player disappears from active lists
Deactivation tests verify player still shows in historical match results
Deactivation tests verify Team Captain guard prevents deactivation
Deactivation tests verify future fixture cleanup occurs
Reactivation tests verify player can be brought back
Season copy tests verify inactive players are excluded
Fantasy auth tests verify inactive player cannot log in
All tests pass in CI Docker environment

approach: 1. Create multi-season-seed.sql with two seasons of fixture/matchup/player data
2. Include players in various states: active both seasons, left after S1, joined in S2, team captain, day captain
3. Write player-lifecycle.spec.ts covering deactivation flow, guard rails, reactivation
4. Write season-scoped-points.spec.ts covering points isolation, departed players, Division 4 rule
5. Use existing test helpers for auth, HTMX interactions, navigation
6. Ensure tests are independent — each test resets to known seed state
7. Verify all tests pass with make test-e2e

points_season_isolation: Seed S1 with completed fixtures and matchups, S2 with no completed fixtures
Verify points table shows zero entries when S2 is active
Complete a fixture in S2, verify only S2 results appear
Verify S1 player who is not on S2 team does not appear
deactivation_flow: Deactivate player, verify removal from player list
Verify deactivated player still appears in completed fixture view
Attempt to deactivate Team Captain, verify blocking error
Reassign captain, then deactivate, verify success
Verify future fixture selections cleaned up
reactivation_flow: Verify player reappears in player list
Verify player is NOT automatically on any team (must be re-added)
season_copy: Deactivate player in S1, copy to S2
Verify inactive player not copied to S2 teams
fantasy_auth: Attempt login with their token, verify failure

testing_requirements: All scenarios listed in test_scenarios pass
Tests run in under 60 seconds total
Tests are parallelisable (no shared mutable state between spec files)
Test output is parseable by parse-results.mjs

context.user_story: As a developer, I need confidence that player removal and season transitions won't break historical data or leak cross-season results
